Description:
  Utilities for converting CQL or ELM into other artefacts, such as C#, .NET assemblies or FHIR Resources. Pick from a command listed below, or type [command] --help for more information on it.
  
  DISCLAIMER:
  The cql command is a very early addition and only supports basic cql translation. It is not yet production ready. If you find issues, please start from the ELM produced by the Java v3.29.0 tooling instead as input for the elm command.

Usage:
  cql-package [command] [options]

Options:
  --log-append                                                               Append to existing log file, instead of clearing
  --console-log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The minimum log level to output to the console
  --file-log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>     The minimum log level to output to file
  --log-file <log-file>                                                      The path of the log file. If not specified, no file logging is performed.
  --profile <profile>                                                        The name of the profile to use from the configuration file. e.g. {App}.{Profile}.appsettings.json.
                                                                             This can be used to load different profiles, for example a name of a library set.
  --version                                                                  Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                                                             Show help and usage information

Commands:
  elm                          Start from ELM and convert to one or more of the following outputs: C#, DLL, PDB, FHIR Resources. When outputing to FHIR Resources, the CQL matchinging against the ELM based on their versioned identifier must be supplied as well.
  
                               DISCLAIMER:
                               The cql command is a very early addition and only supports basic cql translation. It is not yet production ready. If you find issues, please start from the ELM produced by the Java v3.29.0 tooling instead as input for the elm command.
  
  
                               Exit Codes:
                                 0 - Success
                                 2  - No ELM libraries found in the ELM input directory
                                 3  - No ELM libraries compiled
                                 4  - Cannot package: No matching CQL-ELM pairs found
                                 5  - No output directories specified
                                 6  - CQL directory is required when FHIR output is requested
                                 13 - No CQL libraries found in the CQL input directory (when FHIR output is requested)
                                 7  - If --pdb is specified, then --debug-symbols must be PortablePdb
                                 8  - If --pdb is specified, then --dll is required
                                 9  - If --debug-symbols is PortablePdb, then (--dll with --pdb) or (--fhir) or (--libraries with --measures) must be specified
                                 17 - Cannot mix --fhir with --libraries or --measures
                                 18 - Both --libraries and --measures must be specified together
                                 19 - Failed to set up the log file
  cql                          Start from CQL and convert to one or more of the following outputs: ELM, C#, DLL, PDB, FHIR Resources. Take note of the disclaimer above.
  
                               DISCLAIMER:
                               The cql command is a very early addition and only supports basic cql translation. It is not yet production ready. If you find issues, please start from the ELM produced by the Java v3.29.0 tooling instead as input for the elm command.
  
  
                               Exit Codes:
                                 0 - Success
                                 1  - No CQL libraries found in the CQL input directory
                                 3  - No ELM libraries compiled
                                 4  - Cannot package: No matching CQL-ELM pairs found
                                 5  - No output directories specified
                                 7  - If --pdb is specified, then --debug-symbols must be PortablePdb
                                 8  - If --pdb is specified, then --dll is required
                                 9  - If --debug-symbols is PortablePdb, then (--dll with --pdb) or (--fhir) or (--libraries with --measures) must be specified
                                 16 - No CQL libraries converted to ELM
                                 17 - Cannot mix --fhir with --libraries or --measures
                                 18 - Both --libraries and --measures must be specified together
                                 19 - Failed to set up the log file
  extract-library-attachments  ALPHA FEATURE: This command is currently in alpha and its behavior may change in future releases.
  
                               Extract attachments from a FHIR Library resource to individual files. Given a FHIR Library resource file, this command extracts embedded attachments (CQL, ELM, C#, DLL, PDB) and saves them to the specified output directories.
  
  
                               Exit Codes:
                                 0 - Success
                                 5  - No output directories specified
                                 10 - Library extraction error occurred
                                 15 - FHIR Library must have a Name property
                                 19 - Failed to set up the log file
  replace-library-attachments  ALPHA FEATURE: This command is currently in alpha and its behavior may change in future releases.
  
                               Replace attachments in an existing FHIR library resource. Given a FHIR library file and one or more attachment files, this command replaces or adds the corresponding content in the library (identified by content type: +cql, +elm, +dll, +pdb, +csharp). If --library-out-file is not specified, the library-file will be updated in-place. If --library-out-file is specified, library-file will be copied to library-out-file, and the latter will be updated.
  
                               Note: When replacing the ELM attachment (--elm-file), the CQL options parameters in the FHIR Library resource will be automatically overridden with the values from the new ELM file.
  
  
                               Exit Codes:
                                 0 - Success
                                 11 - No input files specified or one or more input files do not exist
                                 12 - Invalid library JSON
                                 14 - Invalid FHIR library, missing required Name and Version
                                 19 - Failed to set up the log file
                                 99 - Unknown error occurred during processing

Description:
  ALPHA FEATURE: This command is currently in alpha and its behavior may change in future releases.
  
  Extract attachments from a FHIR Library resource to individual files. Given a FHIR Library resource file, this command extracts embedded attachments (CQL, ELM, C#, DLL, PDB) and saves them to the specified output directories.
  
  
  Exit Codes:
    0 - Success
    5  - No output directories specified
    10 - Library extraction error occurred
    15 - FHIR Library must have a Name property
    19 - Failed to set up the log file

Usage:
  cql-package extract-library-attachments [options]

Options:
  --library-file <library-file> (REQUIRED)                                   FHIR Library resource file in JSON format containing embedded attachments.
  --cql-dir <cql-dir>                                                        CQL output directory for extracted CQL files "*.cql".
                                                                             (At least one output directory must be specified)
  --elm-dir <elm-dir>                                                        ELM output directory for extracted ELM JSON files "*.json".
                                                                             (At least one output directory must be specified)
  --csharp-dir <csharp-dir>                                                  C# output directory for extracted C# source code files "*.g.cs".
                                                                             (At least one output directory must be specified)
  --dll-dir <dll-dir>                                                        DLL output directory for extracted .NET assembly libraries "*.dll".
                                                                             (At least one output directory must be specified)
  --pdb-dir <pdb-dir>                                                        PDB output directory for extracted portable debug symbol files "*.pdb".
                                                                             (At least one output directory must be specified)
  --log-append                                                               Append to existing log file, instead of clearing
  --console-log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The minimum log level to output to the console
  --file-log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>     The minimum log level to output to file
  --log-file <log-file>                                                      The path of the log file. If not specified, no file logging is performed.
  --profile <profile>                                                        The name of the profile to use from the configuration file. e.g. {App}.{Profile}.appsettings.json.
                                                                             This can be used to load different profiles, for example a name of a library set.
  -?, -h, --help                                                             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  ALPHA FEATURE: This command is currently in alpha and its behavior may change in future releases.
  
  Replace attachments in an existing FHIR library resource. Given a FHIR library file and one or more attachment files, this command replaces or adds the corresponding content in the library (identified by content type: +cql, +elm, +dll, +pdb, +csharp). If --library-out-file is not specified, the library-file will be updated in-place. If --library-out-file is specified, library-file will be copied to library-out-file, and the latter will be updated.
  
  Note: When replacing the ELM attachment (--elm-file), the CQL options parameters in the FHIR Library resource will be automatically overridden with the values from the new ELM file.
  
  
  Exit Codes:
    0 - Success
    11 - No input files specified or one or more input files do not exist
    12 - Invalid library JSON
    14 - Invalid FHIR library, missing required Name and Version
    19 - Failed to set up the log file
    99 - Unknown error occurred during processing

Usage:
  cql-package replace-library-attachments [options]

Options:
  --library-file <library-file> (REQUIRED)                                   FHIR library file in JSON format to read from.
  --library-out-file <library-out-file>                                      Output file for the updated library. If not specified, the library-file will be updated in-place.
                                                                             If specified, library-file will be copied to this location, and this file will be updated with the new attachments.
  --cql-file <cql-file>                                                      CQL file to replace or add as +cql content in the library.
  --elm-file <elm-file>                                                      ELM file in JSON format to replace or add as +elm content in the library.
                                                                             Note: When this is replaced, the CQL options parameters in the FHIR Library will be overridden with values from the new ELM file.
  --csharp-file <csharp-file>                                                C# source file to replace or add as +csharp content in the library.
  --dll-file <dll-file>                                                      DLL file to replace or add as +dll content in the library.
  --pdb-file <pdb-file>                                                      PDB file to replace or add as +pdb content in the library.
  --json-pretty                                                              Output JSON using multiline and indentation.
  --log-append                                                               Append to existing log file, instead of clearing
  --console-log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The minimum log level to output to the console
  --file-log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>     The minimum log level to output to file
  --log-file <log-file>                                                      The path of the log file. If not specified, no file logging is performed.
  --profile <profile>                                                        The name of the profile to use from the configuration file. e.g. {App}.{Profile}.appsettings.json.
                                                                             This can be used to load different profiles, for example a name of a library set.
  -?, -h, --help                                                             Show help and usage information
